---
title: Usage
subtitle: How to use the Leaflet shortcode extension
leaflet:
  map-basic:
    center: [51.505, -0.09]
    zoom: 13
    height: "400px"
  map-markers:
    center: [59.33, 18.07]
    zoom: 12
    height: "400px"
    markers:
      - lat: 59.33
        lon: 18.07
        popup: "<b>Stockholm</b><br>Capital of Sweden"
        tooltip: "Stockholm"
      - lat: 59.35
        lon: 18.10
        popup: "Djurgården"
      - lat: 59.31
        lon: 18.05
        popup: "Södermalm"
  map-markers-tab:
    center: [59.33, 18.07]
    zoom: 12
    height: "400px"
    markers-file: "data/stockholm-markers.tsv"
    markers-sep: "\t"
  map-markers-csv:
    center: [59.33, 18.07]
    zoom: 12
    height: "400px"
    markers-file: "data/stockholm-markers.csv"
  map-icons:
    center: [48.86, 2.35]
    zoom: 13
    height: "400px"
    markers:
      - lat: 48.8584
        lon: 2.2945
        popup: "<b>Eiffel Tower</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-house"
        icon-color: "tomato"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 48.8606
        lon: 2.3376
        tooltip: "Louvre Museum"
        popup: "<b>Louvre Museum</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-building-columns"
        icon-color: "#4a90d9"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 48.853
        lon: 2.3499
        popup: "<b>Notre-Dame</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-church"
        icon-color: "green"
        icon-size: 16
  map-fontawesome:
    center: [41.9028, 12.4964]
    zoom: 13
    height: "400px"
    markers:
      - lat: 41.9028
        lon: 12.4964
        popup: "<b>Roman Forum</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-landmark"
        icon-color: "#b04a2b"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 41.89
        lon: 12.4922
        tooltip: "Colosseum"
        popup: "<b>Colosseum</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-torii-gate"
        icon-color: "#5c6f7b"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 41.8986
        lon: 12.4768
        popup: "<b>Piazza Navona</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-tree"
        icon-color: "#2f7d32"
        icon-size: 16
  map-options:
    center: [40.7128, -74.006]
    zoom: 14
    height: "400px"
    zoomControl: false
    scrollWheelZoom: false
    dragging: true
    tile:
      url: "https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png"
      attribution: "&copy; OpenStreetMap contributors &copy; CARTO"
      subdomains: "abcd"
      maxZoom: 20
    markers:
      - lat: 40.7128
        lon: -74.006
        popup: "<b>New York City</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-house"
        icon-color: "#ff6b6b"
        icon-size: 20
  map-tile-topplus:
    center: [40.7128, -74.006]
    zoom: 11
    height: "400px"
    tile:
      url: "https://sgx.geodatenzentrum.de/wmts_topplus_open/tile/1.0.0/web/default/WEBMERCATOR/{z}/{y}/{x}.png"
      attribution: "Map data: &copy; <a href=\"https://www.govdata.de/dl-de/by-2-0\">dl-de/by-2-0</a>"
      maxZoom: 16
  map-tile-cartodb:
    center: [40.7128, -74.006]
    zoom: 11
    height: "400px"
    tile:
      url: "https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/rastertiles/voyager/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png"
      attribution: "&copy; <a href=\"https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright\">OpenStreetMap</a> contributors &copy; <a href=\"https://carto.com/attributions\">CARTO</a>"
      maxZoom: 16
format:
  html: 
    code-overflow: wrap
    number-sections: true
---

## Overview

The `leaflet` shortcode embeds interactive Leaflet maps into Quarto documents. It supports two input modes:

1. **YAML metadata** — define map configuration in the document frontmatter and reference by label
2. **Inline shortcode arguments** — pass parameters directly in the shortcode

`center` can be omitted when marker coordinates are available. In that case,
the center is computed as the midpoint of the bounding box of all marker
coordinates (i.e. the midpoint between the extreme latitudes and the extreme
longitudes). This is unbiased with respect to point density — a dense cluster
has no more influence than a single isolated point. If `zoom` is omitted or
cannot be parsed, the shortcode uses `13`.

## Basic map

### YAML metadata approach

Define the map in the YAML frontmatter:

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-basic:
    center: [51.505, -0.09]
    zoom: 13
    height: "400px"
---
```

Then reference it in the document:

```lua
{{{< leaflet map-basic >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-basic >}}

### Inline approach

Pass parameters directly as shortcode arguments:

```lua
{{{< leaflet center="[48.86, 2.35]" zoom=14 height="350px" >}}}
```

{{< leaflet center="[48.86, 2.35]" zoom=14 height="350px" >}}

## Markers

Add markers with popups and tooltips:

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-markers:
    center: [59.33, 18.07]
    zoom: 12
    height: "400px"
    markers:
      - lat: 59.33
        lon: 18.07
        popup: "<b>Stockholm</b><br>Capital of Sweden"
        tooltip: "Stockholm"
      - lat: 59.35
        lon: 18.10
        popup: "Djurgården"
      - lat: 59.31
        lon: 18.05
        popup: "Södermalm"
---

{{{< leaflet map-markers >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-markers >}}

### Inline approach

Markers can also be passed directly as a JSON string:

```lua
{{{< leaflet center="[59.33, 18.07]" zoom=12 height="350px" markers='[{"lat":59.33,"lon":18.07,"popup":"<b>Stockholm</b>","tooltip":"Stockholm"},{"lat":59.35,"lon":18.10,"popup":"Djurgården"},{"lat":59.31,"lon":18.05,"popup":"Södermalm"}]' >}}}
```

{{< leaflet center="[59.33, 18.07]" zoom=12 height="350px" markers='[{"lat":59.33,"lon":18.07,"popup":"<b>Stockholm</b>","tooltip":"Stockholm"},{"lat":59.35,"lon":18.10,"popup":"Djurgården"},{"lat":59.31,"lon":18.05,"popup":"Södermalm"}]' >}}

`center` can be omitted when markers are present:

```lua
{{{< leaflet height="350px" markers='[{"lat":59.33,"lon":18.07,"popup":"<b>Stockholm</b>","tooltip":"Stockholm"},{"lat":59.35,"lon":18.10,"popup":"Djurgården"},{"lat":59.31,"lon":18.05,"popup":"Södermalm"}]' >}}}
```

{{< leaflet height="350px" markers='[{"lat":59.33,"lon":18.07,"popup":"<b>Stockholm</b>","tooltip":"Stockholm"},{"lat":59.35,"lon":18.10,"popup":"Djurgården"},{"lat":59.31,"lon":18.05,"popup":"Södermalm"}]' >}}

### Delimited text file approach

Markers can be read from any text file at render time using `markers-file`. The file must include `lat` and `lon` columns. Optional columns include `popup`, `tooltip`, `icon`, `icon-color`, `icon-size`, `icon-anchor`, and `icon-url`.

```yaml
leaflet:
  map-markers-tab:
    center: [59.33, 18.07]
    zoom: 12
    height: "400px"
    markers-file: "data/stockholm-markers.tsv"
    markers-sep: "\t"
```

```tsv
lat	lon	popup	tooltip	icon	icon-color	icon-size
59.33	18.07	<b>Stockholm</b><br>Capital of Sweden	Stockholm	fa-solid fa-house	tomato	18
59.35	18.10	Djurgården	Djurgården	fa-solid fa-tree	#2f7d32	18
59.31	18.05	Södermalm	Södermalm	fa-solid fa-briefcase	#4a90d9	18
```

```lua
{{{< leaflet map-markers-tab >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-markers-tab >}}

Inline shortcodes can use the same file:

```lua
{{{< leaflet center="[59.33, 18.07]" zoom=12 height="350px" markers-file="data/stockholm-markers.tsv" markers-sep="\t" >}}}
```

{{< leaflet center="[59.33, 18.07]" zoom=12 height="350px" markers-file="data/stockholm-markers.tsv" markers-sep="\t" >}}

`center` is also optional for file-based markers:

```lua
{{{< leaflet height="350px" markers-file="data/stockholm-markers.tsv" markers-sep="\t" >}}}
```

{{< leaflet height="350px" markers-file="data/stockholm-markers.tsv" markers-sep="\t" >}}

### CSV file approach

CSV files can be loaded directly with `markers-file`. If omitted, separator
defaults to comma for `.csv`, tab for `.tsv`, and tab otherwise.

```yaml
leaflet:
  map-markers-csv:
    center: [59.33, 18.07]
    zoom: 12
    height: "400px"
    markers-file: "data/stockholm-markers.csv"
```

```csv
lat,lon,popup,tooltip,icon,icon-color,icon-size
59.33,18.07,"<b>Stockholm</b><br>Capital of Sweden",Stockholm,fa-solid fa-house,tomato,26
59.35,18.10,Djurgården,Djurgården,fa-solid fa-tree,#2f7d32,26
59.31,18.05,Södermalm,Södermalm,fa-solid fa-briefcase,#4a90d9,26
```

```lua
{{{< leaflet map-markers-csv >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-markers-csv >}}

Inline shortcodes can also read CSV files:

```lua
{{{< leaflet center="[59.33, 18.07]" zoom=12 height="350px" markers-file="data/stockholm-markers.csv" >}}}
```

{{< leaflet center="[59.33, 18.07]" zoom=12 height="350px" markers-file="data/stockholm-markers.csv" >}}

## Icon markers

Use [Font Awesome 6 classes](https://fontawesome.com/v6/search) for markers. The extension now loads the stylesheet automatically, so no extra header setup is required.

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-icons:
    center: [48.86, 2.35]
    zoom: 13
    height: "400px"
    markers:
      - lat: 48.8584
        lon: 2.2945
        popup: "<b>Eiffel Tower</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-house"
        icon-color: "tomato"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 48.8606
        lon: 2.3376
        tooltip: "Louvre Museum"
        popup: "<b>Louvre Museum</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-building-columns"
        icon-color: "#4a90d9"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 48.853
        lon: 2.3499
        popup: "<b>Notre-Dame</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-church"
        icon-color: "green"
        icon-size: 16
---

{{{< leaflet map-icons >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-icons >}}

### Font Awesome example

Use Font Awesome classes in `icon`. The extension injects the stylesheet automatically.

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-fontawesome:
    center: [41.9028, 12.4964]
    zoom: 13
    height: "400px"
    markers:
      - lat: 41.9028
        lon: 12.4964
        popup: "<b>Roman Forum</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-landmark"
        icon-color: "#b04a2b"
        icon-size: 16
      - lat: 41.89
        lon: 12.4922
        tooltip: "Colosseum"
        popup: "<b>Colosseum</b>"
        icon: "fa-solid fa-torii-gate"
        icon-color: "#5c6f7b"
        icon-size: 16
---

{{{< leaflet map-fontawesome >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-fontawesome >}}

### Icon types

| Mode | Example | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Font Awesome | `icon: "fa-solid fa-tree"` | Included automatically by the extension. Use any [Font Awesome](https://fontawesome.com/icons/) class name. |
| Custom image | `icon: {url: "pin.png", size: [25,41], anchor: [12,41]}` | Any image file. |
| Default | *(omit `icon`)* | Standard Leaflet blue marker. |

Additional icon options:

- `icon-color`: color of the location-pin background icon behind icon-font markers; the glyph itself is white (default: `"currentColor"`)
- `icon-size`: base size in pixels for icon-font markers; scales both the white glyph and its location-pin background (default: `14`)
- `icon-anchor`: `[x, y]` anchor point override
- `markers-sep`: field separator for `markers-file` (for example `","`, `"\t"`, `"|"`)

## Passthrough options

**Any Leaflet [Map option](https://leafletjs.com/reference.html#map-option)** can be passed through. Options must be written in camelCase exactly as Leaflet expects them (e.g. `scrollWheelZoom`, `zoomControl`).

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-options:
    center: [40.7128, -74.006]
    zoom: 14
    height: "400px"
    zoomControl: false
    scrollWheelZoom: false
    dragging: true
---

{{{< leaflet map-options >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-options >}}

## Custom tile layers

Specify a custom tile provider via the `tile` sub-object. See this [demo](https://leaflet-extras.github.io/leaflet-providers/preview/) or [this](https://alexurquhart.github.io/free-tiles/) for examples of tile provider URLs and attribution strings.

### TopPlusOpen

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-tile-topplus:
    center: [40.7128, -74.006]
    zoom: 11
    height: "400px"
    tile:
      url: "https://sgx.geodatenzentrum.de/wmts_topplus_open/tile/1.0.0/web/default/WEBMERCATOR/{z}/{y}/{x}.png"
      attribution: "Map data: &copy; <a href=\"https://www.govdata.de/dl-de/by-2-0\">dl-de/by-2-0</a>"
      maxZoom: 16
---

{{{< leaflet map-tile-topplus >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-tile-topplus >}}

### CartoDB Voyager

```yaml
---
leaflet:
  map-tile-cartodb:
    center: [40.7128, -74.006]
    zoom: 11
    height: "400px"
    tile:
      url: "https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/rastertiles/voyager/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png"
      attribution: "&copy; <a href=\"https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright\">OpenStreetMap</a> contributors &copy; <a href=\"https://carto.com/attributions\">CARTO</a>"
      maxZoom: 16
---

{{{< leaflet map-tile-cartodb >}}}
```

{{< leaflet map-tile-cartodb >}}

### ESRI World Topo Map

```lua
{{{< leaflet center="[40.7128, -74.006]" zoom=11 height="400px" tile='{"url":"https://server.arcgisonline.com/ArcGIS/rest/services/World_Topo_Map/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}","attribution":"Tiles &copy; ESRI"}' >}}}
```

{{< leaflet center="[40.7128, -74.006]" zoom=11 height="400px" tile='{"url":"https://server.arcgisonline.com/ArcGIS/rest/services/World_Topo_Map/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}","attribution":"Tiles &copy; ESRI"}' >}}

## Multiple maps

You can define multiple maps in the same document. Each map gets a unique ID automatically:

```lua
{{{< leaflet center="[35.68, 139.69]" zoom=10 height="300px" >}}}
{{{< leaflet center="[-33.87, 151.21]" zoom=12 height="300px" >}}}
```

{{< leaflet center="[35.68, 139.69]" zoom=10 height="300px" >}}

{{< leaflet center="[-33.87, 151.21]" zoom=12 height="300px" >}}

See the repo [README](https://github.com/royfrancis/quarto-leaflet) for full list of arguments.
